title: How Nikola Tesla Helped Build the Electrical World We Live In

lesson.learn:
Nikola Tesla did not single-handedly create modern electricity, but he helped make the electrical system we use today practical at scale. His work on alternating current, motors, transformers, and high-voltage systems helped move electricity from a curiosity into a reliable infrastructure. That infrastructure made long-distance power transmission much more workable.

Tesla was also a showman and a visionary. He imagined wireless power, dramatic high-voltage experiments, and a future where electricity would be everywhere. Some of his ideas were ahead of their time, but many of his engineering contributions became essential to the modern electric age.
lesson.simpler: Tesla helped make electricity useful on a big scale by improving alternating current systems and inventing important electrical devices.
lesson.keyTakeaway: Tesla mattered because he turned electricity into a practical system for homes, factories, and cities.
lesson.deeperDive:
Tesla's greatest influence was on alternating current, which made it easier to send power over long distances with manageable losses. His motors and transformers fit into a much larger system that included other inventors and engineers, especially the industrial and financial support that made large electrical grids possible. The famous Tesla coil became a symbol of that era's fascination with high-voltage power.

The deeper lesson is that modern technology is almost never the work of one genius alone. Tesla was brilliant, but his impact came from ideas that could be built, scaled, and adopted by an entire electrical civilization.
lesson.funFact: Tesla's coil became a symbol of the electric age.
references: Tesla Science Center - Wardenclyffe and AC

people: Nikola Tesla - inventor and electrical engineer
George Westinghouse - helped commercialize AC power
Engineers - people who built the grid
places: Colorado Springs - Tesla's famous high-voltage experiments
Wardenclyffe - the wireless power project site
Power stations - places where AC systems scaled up
Cities and factories - the places electrification transformed
years: 1890s - AC power systems win the current war
1901-1905 - Wardenclyffe is built and then falters
Present day - Tesla's name remains linked to electricity
concepts: Alternating current - electricity that reverses direction
Transformer - a device that changes voltage
Motor - a machine that turns electricity into motion
High voltage - electricity at large potential difference
Electrification - the spread of electrical power into daily life
